response.justification: Candidate has strong geospatial domain experience (founded GISCollective, contributed to Green Map) and systems-level work in DLang at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby including low-latency async I/O, fibers, and high-throughput services — concepts transferable to a routing pipeline. However, the role's primary requirement is modern C++ (or Rust), and the resume shows only a brief ~6-month C/C++ stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015, with no Rust and a recent stack dominated by TypeScript/Node.js. Worth a screen to gauge C++ depth and willingness to retool, but not a strong match on the core technical requirement.
strengths: Genuine geospatial/GIS background through GISCollective and Green Map collaboration
Systems-programming mindset from DLang work on low-latency, high-throughput microservices
Linux fluency, DevOps experience, and a track record of cross-stack learning
concerns: Minimal modern C++ experience (one short role a decade ago) and no Rust
Recent years focused on TypeScript/Node.js web platforms rather than data pipelines or systems code
No demonstrated experience with navigation/routing systems or large-scale distributed data pipelines
